title: Agent Control Contract — Capabilities Manifest
description: The neutral, machine-readable capabilities manifest: the discovery spine of the Agent Control Contract (ACC). A conformant agent emits this object from the one-shot `--capabilities` entrypoint and (later) from the live `agent://capabilities` resource. agentctl consumes ONLY this contract, never an agent-specific shape (principle P0). The `surfaces{}` block is the SINGLE discovery point for every control-plane surface (RFC 0014 §6.2): a key ABSENT means the surface is unbuilt and the consumer degrades gracefully; never branch on a value that is not advertised here. VERSIONING (RFC 0014 §6.3): `contract_version` is major.minor; additive growth (new fields/tools/metrics/surfaces keys) bumps MINOR and a consumer MUST tolerate it (this object and its open sub-objects use additionalProperties:true); a consumer refuses ONLY an unknown MAJOR. DE-BRANDING (P0): the reference implementation emits agentd-branded spellings (agentd_version key, agentd:// scheme, agentd_ metric prefix, AGENTD_* env). The contract canonicalizes neutral tokens (agent_version, agent://, agent_, AGENT_*) and keeps the branded spellings as accepted aliases; cutover is a GA decision and branded forms are NOT dropped while fielded agents emit them. SECRETS: this manifest NEVER carries credentials (RFC 0012 §3.7) — intelligence is structural (transport scheme + endpoint count) only.

anyOf.description: Branded reference-impl alias (emitted by the reference agent today).

contract_version.description: The Agent Control Contract version this binary speaks, major.minor (reference CONTRACT_VERSION = "1.0"). Negotiation is on MAJOR only: a consumer refuses an instance whose MAJOR it does not understand and tolerates any unknown additive content within a MAJOR it knows.

agent_version.description: NEUTRAL build/version string of the agent binary (de-branded from agentd_version). Free-form, impl-defined (reference emits CARGO_PKG_VERSION). De-branding compat: at least one of agent_version / agentd_version MUST be present (anyOf at the root).

agentd_version.description: BRANDED ALIAS of agent_version. The reference implementation emits THIS key today; the contract accepts it until the GA cutover. Consumers SHOULD prefer agent_version when both are present.

build_features.description: OPAQUE list of compile-time features present in this binary (reference tokens include tls, vsock, serve-mcp, a2a, cron, metrics, otel). The contract does NOT freeze the membership of this set and consumers MUST NOT branch on individual values — feature presence is advertised structurally via surfaces{}. Treat build_features as diagnostic metadata only; new tokens are additive and must be tolerated.

mode.description: The configured run shape. NOT a frozen enum (open, additive-tolerant): the reference set is once | loop | reactive | schedule, but a consumer MUST tolerate an unknown mode string.

model.description: Operator-declared model identifier, or null when unset. Metadata only, NEVER a secret (RFC 0012 §3.7).

mcp_servers.description: Operator-declared MCP client servers, structural name + trifecta tags only — never the spawn command/argv (RFC 0012 §3.1).

a2a_peers.description: Operator-declared A2A delegation peers, structural name + transport scheme only — never the endpoint path/cid (RFC 0020 §3).

exec_enabled.description: Whether the local exec/tool surface is enabled for this run.

allow_trifecta.description: Whether the lethal-trifecta combination is permitted (RFC 0012). Additive over the RFC 0014 §5 spine sketch; present in the reference source and RFC 0015 §5.2.

identity.description: Downward-API identity block (RFC 0014 §6.4, RFC 0015 §6). DESCRIPTIVE only — never load-bearing for placement/scheduling. run_id is always present (a ULID is synthesized if unset); the Kubernetes-sourced fields are string-or-null. Reference reads these from the downward-API env vars noted per field (neutral AGENT_* with the branded AGENTD_* alias accepted until GA); an empty env value is coerced to unset (null).

run_id.description: Always present. Stable run identity (ULID if not supplied). Reference env: AGENT_RUN_ID (alias AGENTD_RUN_ID).

instance.description: Pod name. Downward API metadata.name via AGENT_POD_NAME (alias AGENTD_POD_NAME). null when unset.

uid.description: Pod UID. Downward API metadata.uid via AGENT_POD_UID (alias AGENTD_POD_UID). null when unset.

node.description: Node name. Downward API spec.nodeName via AGENT_NODE_NAME (alias AGENTD_NODE_NAME). null when unset.

namespace.description: Namespace. Downward API metadata.namespace via AGENT_POD_NAMESPACE (alias AGENTD_POD_NAMESPACE). null when unset.

intelligence.description: STRUCTURAL intelligence summary — transport scheme + configured endpoint count + reachability. NEVER an endpoint URL or token (no embedded credentials, RFC 0012 §3.7). The full per-endpoint health view lives at agent://intelligence; the manifest carries only the bounded count.

transport.description: The PRIMARY endpoint's transport scheme, or null when no endpoint is configured / scheme is unrecognized. Reference scheme set: unix | vsock | https.

endpoints.description: Count of configured intelligence endpoints (0 when unset; a single --intelligence element is 1).

healthy.description: SUM TYPE: a boolean last-known reachability, OR the literal string "unknown" on the one-shot pre-connect probe (nothing has connected yet). Consumers MUST handle both the boolean and the "unknown" string; codegen needs a hand-written deserializer for this field.

intelligence_summary.description: Coarse capability hints for placement (no secrets, RFC 0012). Open object; fields are advisory and may be omitted. Reference emits only `toolmode`; `max_context_hint` is omitted unless configured.

max_context_hint.description: Operator-declared max-context hint, or null. Omitted-when-absent in the reference.

mcp_server.description: An MCP client server entry: structural name + trifecta tags only.

a2a_peer.description: An A2A delegation peer entry: structural name + transport scheme only (no address).

transport.description: Peer transport scheme. Reference set: vsock | unix | unknown (unknown when the endpoint scheme is unrecognized).

limits.description: The bounding box (RFC 0007/0009/0003): per-run limits + supervisor fork-bomb tree caps. All values are integers. Open object; consumers tolerate additional limit keys.

max_children.description: Max direct children per node (tree cap).

max_total_subagents.description: Max total subagents in the tree (tree cap).

deadline_ms.description: Wall-clock deadline in ms (a large sentinel when no deadline is set).

surfaces.description: The graceful-degradation contract and SINGLE discovery point (RFC 0014 §6.2 / §8). Each control-plane surface is reported HONESTLY as served-or-not for THIS build/config; agentctl drives ONLY what is declared here. A key ABSENT means the surface is unbuilt — degrade gracefully; do NOT treat absence as an error. additionalProperties:true is mandatory (additive-tolerance): NEVER apply deny-unknown / additionalProperties:false here — new surface keys are additive and bump MINOR. Several keys are SUM TYPES (bool|string, bool|object) and require hand-written deserializers in any codegen target (agentctl RFC 0018 §3.3): codegen cannot derive these.

management.description: SUM TYPE (bool|string): the served self-MCP management transport address when configured (e.g. "vsock:PORT" or "unix:PATH"), else false (RFC 0015 §3). The reference maps Option<String> to a JSON string or the boolean false; codegen needs a hand-written deserializer.

operator_tools.description: Operator tools advertised on the management transport, mirroring tools/list for a Management peer (RFC 0015 §4 / §5.2). The reference v1 set, in FROZEN order, is: drain, lame-duck, pause, resume, cancel (source OPERATOR_TOOLS). `attach` is NOT a tool — it maps to subagent.send and is excluded. There is NO `force` tool in v1 (force = a second SIGTERM). Empty array when the build cannot serve the management transport. Items are typed as open strings (not a closed enum) for additive-tolerance: a consumer MUST tolerate an unknown additive tool name and refuse only at a new MAJOR.

a2a.description: SUM TYPE (bool|object): the A2A external-agent surface (RFC 0020). false when this build does not serve A2A; otherwise an object advertising the served method set. PROVISIONAL (P2): this key is NOT in the RFC 0015 §5.2 frozen sketch but IS emitted by the reference a2a build and required by RFC 0020 — included here, binding open. WIRE-STRING BINDING OPEN (P2): `methods` records the reference PascalCase a2a.* spelling; the A2A-spec slash-form (message/send, tasks/get, tasks/cancel, message/stream, tasks/resubscribe, tasks/list) is the alternative binding — the normative spelling is deferred to a later contract decision and a gateway translates until then. Requires a hand-written deserializer (bool|object).

version.description: A2A protocol version this surface advertises (reference "1.0").

streaming.description: Whether status-level streaming (SendStreamingMessage + SubscribeToTask, same-id StreamResponse frames terminated by statusUpdate.final) is served.

methods.description: The served method names. Reference (PascalCase over JSON-RPC NDJSON) v1 set: a2a.SendMessage, a2a.GetTask, a2a.CancelTask, a2a.ListTasks, a2a.SendStreamingMessage, a2a.SubscribeToTask. A2A-spec slash-form alternative (binding open, P2): message/send, tasks/get, tasks/cancel, tasks/list, message/stream, tasks/resubscribe. Open string items for additive-tolerance.

address.description: OPTIONAL dedicated A2A listener address. OMITTED when A2A shares the management listener (the reference behavior); present only when the agent serves A2A on its own transport (P2 sub-question).

metrics.description: SUM TYPE (bool|string): the Prometheus /metrics scrape address when configured (e.g. ":9090"), else false (RFC 0010 / 0016). Carries the ADDRESS only — the byte-identical Prom-text resource (agent://metrics) and the capacity schema (agent://capacity) are out of manifest scope and undefined upstream (P4, downstream dependency). Requires a hand-written deserializer (bool|string).

metrics_schema.description: FROZEN metrics-schema version, major.minor (reference METRICS_SCHEMA = "1.0"). Opaque version string: it gates which metric registry the consumer reads; the autoscaling metric-NAME reconciliation (P10) is resolved in the metrics registry extraction, not here.

events.description: Whether the agent://events stream is served (RFC 0016 §7).

report_schema.description: FROZEN run-outcome report schema version, major.minor (reference REPORT_SCHEMA = "1.0").

exit_codes.description: The exit-code contract version (RFC 0016 §5, around RFC 0011 §5). DIVERGENCE: the RFC 0014/0015 jsonc sketch showed the literal "RFC-0011-§5"; the SOURCE (exit.rs EXIT_CODES) emits a bare version "1.0". The contract PREFERS the source (a major.minor version string); the §-tag is a documentation alias.

intelligence.description: Whether the agent://intelligence endpoint-health resource is served (RFC 0018 §4.4).

hot_reload.description: Whether the SIGHUP-triggered reload of the reloadable config subset is served (RFC 0017 §5).

config_schema.description: Whether --config-schema is available (RFC 0017 §4.2). PROVISIONAL (P6): the reference builder reports true, but the design record §14 flagged --config-schema as not-yet-built; treat a true value as contract-declared rather than independently proven. (Note: the config-file extraction found config_schema() IS implemented in the reference source — see config.schema.json — so this is effectively resolved, kept provisional here for cross-surface consistency.)

cluster.description: Whether horizontal-scaling sharding + the capacity resource are present (RFC 0019). Additive over the RFC 0014 §6.2 key list (present in source).

shard.description: SUM TYPE (string|null): this instance's "K/N" shard identity, or null when unsharded (N==1) or no cluster (RFC 0019).

standby.description: Whether this instance is a standby (--standby); agentctl routes a directed assignment only to instances reporting standby:true (RFC 0019 §7). Additive over the RFC 0014 §6.2 key list (present in source).

claim.description: SUM TYPE (bool|object): when present, the claim styles this instance speaks (RFC 0019 §9 / RFC 0015 §5.6). IMPORTANT: this key is OMITTED-when-absent, never false — a build without the claim path leaves the key out entirely (agentctl places a claim route only on instances advertising it). The schema allows bool|object so that absence validates and a hypothetical false (if ever emitted) still validates, but the contract intent is OMITTED, not false.

styles.description: Claim styles spoken. Reference set: tool, resource.
